key_assumptions.input: Starting free cash flow per share
key_assumptions.source: FY2025 operating cash flow $19.03B less capital spending $1.21B, divided by 1,773M weighted average diluted shares (10-K filed 2026-02-20)
Skyrizi +50% and Rinvoq +39% FY2025 offset Humira -49%; Q1 2026 revenue +12.4% YoY

open_questions: Stance **watch** at ~$250: IRR below 15% hurdle despite quality franchise
No third-party sources indexed; re-run scan when Substacks or fund letters added
January 2026 U.S. government pricing agreement: quantify margin impact when full 8-K terms reviewed
thesis: AbbVie Inc. (NYSE: **ABBV**) is a global biopharmaceutical company built around patented medicines in immunology, oncology, neuroscience, aesthetics, and eye care. **[Fact]** FY2025 net revenues were **$61.2 billion** (up **8.6%** versus **$56.3 billion** in FY2024). **[Fact]** Cash from operations was **$19.0 billion** and capital spending was **$1.2 billion**, yielding about **$10.05** of owner cash per diluted share. **[Fact]** The company reports one operating segment but discloses five therapeutic areas; immunology led by **Skyrizi** and **Rinvoq** is replacing legacy **Humira** revenue lost to biosimilars. **[Inference]** AbbVie is a cash-rich pharma compounder in a deliberate portfolio transition: growth assets are scaling while debt from the Allergan acquisition and a large dividend absorb much of the free cash flow.
why_market_wrong: There is **no classic dated mispricing signal** (HK predictive attribute: **none**). **[Inference]** Generalists may still anchor on Humira's collapse and GAAP net income (**$4.2 billion**, diluted earnings per share **$2.36**) without normalizing for intangible amortization, litigation reserves, and non-operating items. **[Fact]** Skyrizi net revenues rose **50%** in FY2025 to roughly **$17.6 billion** (U.S. **$15.2 billion** plus international **$2.4 billion**), and Rinvoq rose **39%** to roughly **$7.5 billion** globally. **[Fact]** Humira net revenues fell **49%** in FY2025 (U.S. **$3.1 billion** versus **$7.1 billion** prior year). **[Inference]** The market may be paying up for visible dividend growth and immunology momentum, leaving little margin of safety at about **$250** per share on **$10** of owner cash. Return here is **normalized owner cash at price**, not a sum-of-the-parts on pipeline optionality alone.
